prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark frame. Most of the screen is black, with only faint, indistinct shapes near the lower portion; the setting and objects are not clearly visible.

By the final sampled frame, the image has changed to a staged product shot. A large, light-colored side-by-side refrigerator/freezer stands centered against a dark blue, nighttime-looking background with a low mountain or horizon silhouette. The floor or ground surface appears pale and reflective, with bluish-purple lighting. Large white text appears across the bottom of the frame reading: “GUARANTEED UNTIL 2000 A.D.” The refrigerator is the dominant visual object, presented front-facing, with a dark vertical strip and a dispenser-like area visible on the left door.
